MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Eduardo Robles, Esq., of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Ronald Grist

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to punitive damages and off calendar as to false representation. Mr. Walter's declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Seevers was exposed to Kaiser Gypsum joint compound. Defendant failed to satisfy the burden of production as to asbestos content of that joint compound due to the reference in plaintiffs' interrogatory responses to defendant's General Order 129 interrogatory responses. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, evidence warranting the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' special interrogatory responses preclude defendant from invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar on this issue. Plaintiffs dismissed their false representation cause of action. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Mr. Walters and to its General Order interrogatory responses are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Thomas J. Moses, Esq., of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Union Carbide Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing parties George Seevers, et al.

RULING:
UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Union Carbide's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 and 6-8 (sophisticated intermediary defense, bulk supplier defenses, loss of consortium and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 5 (false representation). The sophisticated intermediary defense does not shield defendant from liability. (Stewart). Defendant failed to present evidence showing what knowledge Hamilton Materials had about the hazards of asbestos and what warnings, if any, it provided to Hamilton Materials. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza; Jenkins; Arena). As to punitive damages (issue 7), plaintiffs are not seeking punitive damages based on conduct that occurred in another state. Rather, plaintiffs seek punitive damages based on defendant's mining, supply, sale and distribution of raw asbestos fiber, all of which occurred within California. As to punitive damages (issue 8), there are triable issues whether defendant made misrepresentations about and/or withheld material information concerning its knowledge of the hazards of asbestos and thus whether it acted in conscious disregard for the safety of persons who were exposed to its asbestos. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their cause of action for false representation, although the court sees no dismissal on file. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
